content.full_text:
City of El Segundo

RONALD E. CANO, City Manager

March 6, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan,
General Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request For Attorney General Opinion Concerning
Government Code Section 1090

Dear Ms. Donovan:

The City of El Segundo respectfully requests formal advice pursuant to Section 83114(b) of the Government Code, and Section 18329 of the Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission.

FACTS

A City Planning Commissioner has in the recent past acquired a 16% interest in a newspaper. The newspaper is the adjudicated newspaper for the City of El Segundo which, by statute, must be used by the City of El Segundo for publication of legal notices. The Planning Commissioner votes on a variety of items which require publication of legal notice in this adjudicated newspaper.

The City Council is generally responsible for approving contracts. The purchasing officer and City Manager have limited authority to enter into contracts for purchase of supplies and equipment below specified sums. There is no specific contractual authority for the Planning Commission to enter into contracts in the El Segundo Municipal Code. However, it is clear that the Planning Commission may and does take actions in the course of its duties which require publication of legal notice.

All legal notices which a City is required to publish must be published in a "newspaper of general circulation." (Government Code Section 6040.) Failure to publish in such a newspaper would result in inadequate notice and the possible voidability of any action taken subsequent to such invalid notice. A newspaper of general circulation must be adjudicated as such and must be printed and published in the City where publication is required. (Government Code Section 6000.)

Cities may only publish in a newspaper of general circulation which is published but not printed within its jurisdiction where there is no newspaper of general circulation published within the City. (Government Code Section 6042 but see also Government Code Section 6008.) Failure to observe this requirement may result in personal liability by officials for

350 Main Street, El Segundo, California 90245-0989
Phone (213) 322-4670 FAX (213) 322-7137

Kathryn E. Donovan
California FPPC
March 6, 1990
Page -2-




all damages occasioned by the violation and for liquidated damages of $100 for each
violation of the publication requirements. (Government Code Section 6044.)

Chapter 7 of Title 9 of the Government Code (Government Code Sections 87100 et seq.)
prohibits public officials from making or participating in any way in a government decision
in which the official has a financial interest. (Government Code Sections 87100.) The
term "public official" is broadly defined by Government Code Section 82048 as ". . .every
member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency. . . ."


QUESTIONS RAISED

Is the Planning Commissioner making or participating in a decision in which he has a
financial interest within the meaning of Government Code Section 8700 when he initiates
or votes on a matter which will result in a publication by the City in the newspaper in
which he owns a 16% interest?

We greatly appreciate your attention to and assistance in this matter.

Sincerely yours,


Ronald E. Cano,
City Manager

REC:joa

cc:    Leland C. Dolley, City attorney

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Planning Commissioner who owns a 16% interest in the City's adjudicated newspaper has a financial interest in decisions requiring publication of legal notices in that newspaper under Government Code Section 87100.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request from the City of El Segundo to FPPC requesting formal advice. No FPPC response/conclusion section is present in this document. Document contains detailed factual background and legal citations supporting the question.
